title: SAS at UW-Madison
body: SAS is a data management, multivariate analysis, business intelligence, and predictive analysis suite. Eligibility Facstaff are eligible for SAS access if they are in a University-paid position Students are not eligible for SAS access. This software is not allowed to be used for Commercial Purposes. Any other use violates the terms of our License Agreement with SAS. Our current site license covers Teaching and Research uses. We have a small number of Administrative Licenses available please contact licensing@doit.wisc.edu for more information. For detailed questions about eligibility, see the CSL eligibility KB page: Software Available to Faculty/Staff and Students on Campus Software Library Facstaff access at UW-Madison SAS can be accessed directly through the Campus Software Library after a valid NetID login.  A separate download of the most recent SID File is required for activation. Student access at UW-Madison Students are not eligible for SAS access. What's included in SAS for UW-Madison Version updates and support are included in our license agreement. How to get started with SAS SAS Institute provides online training and getting-started tutorials.  An account needs to be created to access SAS Starter Kit Licensing Models UW-Madison licenses SAS for individual and concurrent installations. SAS License Types: Teaching & Research License: As defined by SAS, is software used for purposes of classroom instruction, academic (non-commercial) research, and course work primarily related to the customer's post-secondary degree requirements, including the formulation of theses and dissertations. May be used by faculty and staff supporting these uses; in the case of academic research, it may be used by faculty, staff, and students on hardware that is owned or leased by the University of Wisconsin - Madison. Administrative Purposes License: As defined by SAS, is software used for purposes directly related to customer's operations as a post-secondary degree granting institution. This includes institutional research and any such use related to reporting about the UW to the UW. Such software may be used by customer's administrative employees (including faculty performing administrative duties) for such administrative purposes. Troubleshooting Software questions and information on how to use the software can be found through the vendor, SAS Support and KB Additional help If you have more questions, please Get Help from DoIT .
